engineIntelligence.h2: Renault Latitude Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Renault Latitude in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Renault Latitude M9R engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo whistle/whining - variable geometry turbo actuator wear causes high-pitched noise from 60k miles.
DPF blockage - frequent short journeys cause particulate filter clogging and limp mode activation.
Connecting rod bearing failure - early production M9R units may suffer bottom-end wear under high load.

cards.altText: Renault Latitude M4R engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ignition coil failure - misfires and rough idle reported on higher-mileage M4R units after 70k miles.
Timing chain tensioner wear - rare but documented on pre-2013 M4R engines with inadequate oil changes.

cards.altText: Renault Latitude V9X engine - diesel 3.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo actuator failure - variable geometry vanes stick on high-mileage V9X units causing power loss.
EGR cooler leaks - documented on V6 diesel Latitudes with over 80k miles, leading to coolant loss.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £2,800-£5,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable V9X units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Renault Latitude Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Renault Latitude across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The M9R 2.0 dCi powered most UK Latitude imports, offering strong torque and efficiency but requiring careful DPF maintenance.
compatibleVariants: Expression 2.0 dCi 150, Dynamique 2.0 dCi 150, Dynamique 2.0 dCi 175, Privilege 2.0 dCi 175, Initiale 2.0 dCi 175
commonFailures: Turbo whistle/whining - VGT actuator wear from 60k miles causes high-pitched noise under load
DPF blockage - short-journey driving leads to particulate filter clogging and limp mode
Connecting rod bearing wear - early M9R production units may suffer bottom-end failure under stress
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R Renault Latitude engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Diesel
entries.history: The flagship V9X 3.0 dCi V6 delivered premium performance for Initiale trim Latitudes, sharing architecture with Nissan platforms.
commonFailures: Turbo actuator failure - variable geometry vanes stick on high-mileage units causing power loss
EGR cooler leaks - documented on V6 diesel Latitudes over 80k miles leading to coolant loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for V9X Renault Latitude engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The M4R 2.0 16V offered smooth, flexible power with flex-fuel capability, popular with UK grey import Latitude owners.
compatibleVariants: Expression 2.0 16V 140, Dynamique 2.0 16V 140, Privilege 2.0 16V Flex-Fuel, Initiale 2.0 16V Flex-Fuel
commonFailures: Ignition coil failure - misfires and rough idle reported on higher-mileage M4R units after 70k miles
Timing chain tensioner wear - rare but documented on pre-2013 M4R engines with inadequate oil changes
entries.cta: Get quotes for M4R Renault Latitude engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: The V4X 2.5 V6 provided refined, torque-rich performance for early Latitude models before being discontinued in the UK.
compatibleVariants: Expression 2.5 V6 180, Dynamique 2.5 V6 180, Privilege 2.5 V6 180
commonFailures: Intake manifold gasket leaks - oil seepage reported on V6 petrol Latitudes over 70k miles
Coil pack failure - intermittent misfires on cylinders under load, especially in humid conditions
entries.cta: Get quotes for V4X Renault Latitude engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Renault Latitude engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Renault Latitude Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Renault Latitude's engine reliability varies by code: the M9R 2.0 dCi dominates UK replacement enquiries due to turbo and DPF issues, while the M4R petrol is generally robust but can suffer ignition faults. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: M9R Turbo Whistle & Power Loss - Why Renault Latitude 2.0 dCi Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Variable geometry turbo actuator wear causes vanes to stick, creating whistle noise and reducing boost pressure.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Actuator Service - Clean and recalibrate VGT mechanism
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Actuator removal, cleaning, bench testing and reprogramming
repairOptions.longevity: May restore function if vanes not seized; not viable if turbo internally damaged

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Replacement - Fit reconditioned turbo unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old turbo, fit reconditioned unit with new gaskets and oil lines
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost and eliminates whistle; addresses symptom not root cause of oil contamination

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M9R Engine - Supply & fit complete unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed engine, fit rebuilt M9R with upgraded turbo, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other wear; upgraded components address original failure points

problems.recommendation: On a Renault Latitude worth £3,000-£6,000, a minor actuator service is worth trying first if the turbo still spins freely. If boost is lost or noise is severe, compare rebuilt engine quotes - the M9R's other wear items may make full replacement more economical long-term.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M9R engine prices for your Renault Latitude

problems.h4: M9R DPF Blockage & Limp Mode - Why Renault Latitude Diesel Owners Face Costly Repairs
problems.affectedModels: Renault Latitude all 2.0 dCi FAP variants (2011-2015, M9R codes with particulate filter)
problems.rootCause: Frequent short journeys prevent DPF regeneration cycles, causing soot accumulation that triggers limp mode and power loss.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - DPF Cleaning & Reset - Off-car cleaning and ECU reset
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove DPF, professional cleaning, reinstall and force regeneration cycle
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if filter not melted or cracked; requires post-service motorway driving

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DPF Replacement - Fit new or reconditioned filter
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove old DPF, fit replacement unit with new sensors and gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Restores emissions compliance; does not address underlying driving pattern causing blockage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M9R Engine - Supply & fit complete unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed engine, fit rebuilt M9R with serviced DPF system, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if engine has high mileage or other faults; upgraded components improve longevity

problems.recommendation: If your Latitude is used mainly for short trips, DPF cleaning may buy 12-18 months. For high-mileage vehicles or those with additional M9R issues, a rebuilt engine with a serviced DPF system offers better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Renault Latitude M9R DPF repair or replacement

problems.h4: M4R Ignition Coil Failure - Why Renault Latitude Petrol Engines Misfire
problems.rootCause: Ignition coils degrade with heat cycles, causing intermittent misfires, rough idle and increased fuel consumption.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Coil Pack Replacement - Replace faulty ignition coils
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnostic scan, remove old coils, fit new OEM-spec coils and reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves misfire if spark plugs and wiring are sound; coils typically last 80k+ miles

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Ignition Service - Coils, plugs, leads and throttle body clean
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all ignition components, clean throttle body and intake, ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive fix for persistent misfires; recommended if vehicle has 90k+ miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M4R Engine - Supply & fit complete unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed engine, fit rebuilt M4R with new ignition system, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has high mileage, oil consumption or other wear beyond ignition

problems.recommendation: For a single-cylinder misfire on a sub-80k mile Latitude, coil replacement is cost-effective. If multiple cylinders misfire or the engine has high mileage, compare rebuilt engine quotes - the M4R is generally robust but labour costs for repeated ignition repairs add up.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M4R engine prices for your Renault Latitude

problems.h4: V9X Turbo Actuator & EGR Issues - Why Renault Latitude V6 Diesel Owners Need Specialist Care
problems.affectedModels: Renault Latitude Initiale 3.0 dCi V6 (2011-2015, V9X 891/892)
problems.rootCause: Twin-turbo VGT actuators stick with age; EGR cooler cracks cause coolant loss and overheating risks.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Actuator Service & EGR Blank - Clean actuators, blank EGR
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbos for actuator cleaning, fit EGR blanking plate, ECU remap
repairOptions.longevity: May restore boost if turbos not damaged; blanking EGR requires emissions compliance check

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo & EGR Replacement - Fit reconditioned turbos and EGR cooler
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove both turbos and EGR system, fit reconditioned units with new gaskets and sensors
repairOptions.longevity: Restores performance and cooling; high labour cost makes this less economical on high-mileage cars

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt V9X Engine - Supply & fit complete unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed engine, fit rebuilt V9X with serviced turbo/EGR system, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for flagship Initiale models; upgraded components address known V9X weaknesses

problems.recommendation: Given the V9X's complexity and the Initiale's residual value, minor actuator service is worth attempting first. If coolant loss or severe power loss occurs, compare rebuilt engine quotes - specialist V9X expertise is essential for reliable repair.
problems.cta: Enquire about V9X Renault Latitude engine replacement from V6 specialists

problems.h4: M9R Connecting Rod Bearing Failure - Why Early Renault Latitude 2.0 dCi Engines Need Attention
problems.affectedModels: Renault Latitude early production 2.0 dCi (2011-2012, M9R 740/742 pre-facelift)
problems.rootCause: Early M9R bearing tolerances combined with oil starvation during hard acceleration can cause bottom-end knock.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil System Flush & Bearing Inspection - Diagnostic borescope check
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Oil drain, flush, borescope inspection of bearings via spark plug hole
repairOptions.longevity: Only diagnostic; cannot repair bearing wear but identifies severity before catastrophic failure

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Bottom-End Rebuild - Replace bearings, inspect crank
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, split block, replace bearings, machine crank if needed, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if crank undamaged; labour-intensive and may not address other M9R wear items

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt M9R Engine - Supply & fit complete unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed engine, fit rebuilt M9R with upgraded bearings and oil pump, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; upgraded components address original bearing design weakness

problems.recommendation: If you hear a deep knock from the bottom end, stop driving immediately - bearing failure can destroy the crank. For early M9R Latitudes over 90k miles, compare rebuilt engine quotes proactively; the upgraded bearings in rebuilt units prevent recurrence.
problems.cta: Get urgent quotes for M9R Renault Latitude engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Renault Latitude
finalCta.paragraph: From turbo whistle to DPF blockage, the Renault Latitude's common engine problems can be managed with the right specialist support. EngineMarket connects you with vetted UK Renault Latitude engine specialists who supply rebuilt, reconditioned and used engines with transparent pricing and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Renault Latitude Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Renault Latitude and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Renault Latitude Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Renault Latitude's UK production timeline (2011-2015), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: L70 Launch: M9R Diesel & M4R Petrol Debut
years.description: The Latitude launched in the UK via grey imports with the M9R 2.0 dCi and M4R 2.0 16V as core engines, plus the V4X 2.5 V6 petrol for early models.
keyChanges: Renault Latitude L70 generation introduced to UK via grey import channels
M9R 2.0 dCi (150/175hp) and M4R 2.0 16V (140hp) confirmed for UK market
V4X 2.5 V6 petrol available on Expression/Dynamique/Privilege trims
Initiale flagship trim launched with optional V9X 3.0 dCi V6
knownFor: Strong executive saloon practicality with competitive running costs
Early M9R units (pre-2012) show higher incidence of connecting rod bearing wear
V4X V6 petrol discontinued after 2013 due to emissions and demand factors
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2012 Renault Latitude replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Latitude 2011 engine replacement • M9R turbo whistle fix UK • Latitude 2.0 dCi bearing failure

years.preview: L70 Matures: M9R Refinements & V9X Flagship Focus
years.description: Post-facelift Latitudes retained the M9R and M4R core engines while focusing the V9X V6 diesel exclusively on the Initiale flagship trim.
keyChanges: Minor facelift updates to interior trim and equipment levels
V4X 2.5 V6 petrol discontinued in UK market after 2013
M9R engine codes updated (744/746/786) with incremental reliability improvements
V9X 3.0 dCi V6 confirmed as exclusive Initiale powertrain for remainder of production
mainEngines: M4R - 2.0 petrol (all trims, flex-fuel capability standard)
knownFor: Improved M9R bearing specifications reduced early-failure risk on post-2013 units
DPF maintenance remains critical for M9R longevity on short-journey cars
V9X V6 diesel commands premium replacement pricing due to rarity and complexity
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2015 Renault Latitude replacement engines
years.ticker: Renault Latitude 2013 engine replacement • M9R DPF blockage fix • Initiale V6 diesel specialist UK

faq.h2: Renault Latitude Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Renault Latitude engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Renault Latitude engine prices range from £1,400 for a 2.0L petrol M4R to £5,500 for the flagship 3.0L V6 diesel V9X, with the common 2.0 dCi M9R averaging £1,500-£3,500 supply-only.
keyPoints: M9R 2.0 dCi rebuilt: £1,500-£3,500 supply-only
M4R 2.0 petrol rebuilt: £1,400-£3,200 supply-only
V9X 3.0 dCi V6 rebuilt: £2,800-£5,500 supply-only
Supply & fit labour adds £400-£900 depending on workshop
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare Renault Latitude engine replacement prices today

items.question: Is the Renault Latitude 2.0 dCi M9R engine reliable?
items.answer: The M9R 2.0 dCi is generally robust but has documented issues with turbo whistle, DPF blockage on short journeys, and rare connecting rod bearing wear on early 2011-2012 units.
keyPoints: Turbo actuator wear causes whistle noise from ~60k miles
DPF requires regular motorway runs to regenerate properly
Early M9R units (pre-2013) had bearing tolerance improvements later
Regular oil changes with correct spec fluid are critical for longevity
Rebuilt M9R engines include upgraded components addressing known weaknesses
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt M9R Renault Latitude engines

items.question: What engines fit the Renault Latitude?
items.answer: Only engines originally fitted to the L70-generation Renault Latitude (2011-2015) are compatible: M9R 2.0 dCi, M4R 2.0 petrol, V4X 2.5 V6 petrol (discontinued), and V9X 3.0 dCi V6.
keyPoints: M4R codes: 730, 735, 746, H7 (2.0 petrol 140hp, flex-fuel)
V9X codes: 891, 892 (3.0 dCi V6 240hp, Initiale only)
Engine code must match exactly for ECU and transmission compatibility
items.cta: Check engine code compatibility for your Renault Latitude

items.question: Where can I find the engine code on my Renault Latitude?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on a metal plate on the engine block, visible near the timing cover or on the cylinder head; it also appears on the V5C logbook under "Engine number".
keyPoints: Look for codes starting M9R, M4R, V4X or V9X on the engine plate
V5C registration document lists engine number under section D.2
Service records and original purchase paperwork often note the code
DVLA lookup via registration confirms engine size and fuel type
Enter your reg on EngineMarket to auto-identify compatible engines
items.cta: Identify your Renault Latitude engine code instantly

items.question: What are the common problems with Renault Latitude engines?
items.answer: M9R diesel engines commonly suffer turbo whistle and DPF blockage; M4R petrol units may experience ignition coil failure; V9X V6 diesels can have turbo actuator and EGR cooler issues.
keyPoints: M9R: Turbo whistle, DPF clogging, rare bearing wear on early units
M4R: Ignition coil failure causing misfires after ~70k miles
V9X: Twin-turbo actuator sticking, EGR cooler leaks on high mileage
V4X: Intake manifold gasket leaks, coil pack issues under load
Regular servicing and appropriate driving patterns mitigate most issues
items.cta: Compare repair vs replacement costs for your Renault Latitude engine

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Renault Latitude?
items.answer: Given the Latitude's residual value (£3,000-£6,000 typical), engine replacement is worthwhile if the car is otherwise sound, has low bodywork damage, and you plan to keep it long-term.
keyPoints: Compare rebuilt engine cost (£1,400-£5,500) against vehicle value
Factor in supply & fit labour (£400-£900) and warranty coverage
Consider selling as-is vs investing in a reliable rebuilt engine
Rebuilt engines with 12-month warranty offer peace of mind
Specialist fitting ensures correct installation and ECU coding
items.cta: Get a tailored quote to decide if replacement makes sense

items.question: Can I fit a used engine in my Renault Latitude?
items.answer: Yes, used Renault Latitude engines are available from UK breakers, but always request compression test results, mileage verification and a minimum 3-6 month warranty before purchase.
keyPoints: Used engines cost £900-£2,800 depending on code and mileage
Always verify donor vehicle history and engine condition reports
Compression test and leak-down test recommended before fitting
Used engines typically carry shorter warranties than rebuilt units
Supply & fit service ensures correct installation and coding
items.cta: Check used Renault Latitude engine availability and warranties

items.question: How long does a Renault Latitude engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engine delivery typically takes 2-5 working days; supply & fit installation requires 1-3 days at a specialist workshop depending on parts availability and booking schedule.
keyPoints: Stock engines (M9R, M4R) often dispatch within 48 hours
Rare engines (V9X, V4X) may require 5-10 days sourcing time
Supply & fit labour typically 1-2 days for experienced specialists
Post-installation ECU adaptation and road test add half a day
Nationwide delivery and collection options available through network
items.cta: Check Renault Latitude engine delivery times and fitting slots

items.question: Do Renault Latitude engine replacements come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty; some specialists offer extended coverage for an additional fee.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt and reconditioned units
Used engines typically include 3-6 month warranty coverage
Warranty covers manufacturing defects and premature component failure
Labour for warranty claims may require fitting at approved workshops
Always confirm exact terms, exclusions and claim process with supplier
items.cta: Compare Renault Latitude engine warranty terms from UK specialists
